Delta Airlines · DL991 / DL752

Your journey begins before you land

Flying Delta means experiencing professional travel firsthand — from navigating LAX to understanding the rhythm of the skies that business leaders live every week. This is Day One of your summit.

6:00 AM · LAX Arrival
Arrive at Los Angeles International Airport. Group meets for check-in and security coordination. Arrive calm, organized, and ready.
TSA Security
Navigate the security checkpoint. Liquids bagged, electronics out, shoes off. Professionals move through without a word.
7:15 AM · Boarding
Gate boarding begins. Boarding pass ready — digital or printed. Listen for your zone. For many, this is a first professional flight. Own it.
8:00 AM · DL991 Departure
Wheels up from Los Angeles. The flight to JFK crosses the country you are about to compete in. Approximately 5.5 hours.
4:29 PM · JFK Arrival
Land at John F. Kennedy International. Charter shuttle to Hyatt Place Midtown Manhattan departs by 5:30 PM.
8:00 PM · DL752 Return (Apr 23)
Departure from JFK. Arrival at LAX at 11:57 PM. The return flight closes a chapter you will carry for the rest of your career.
Visit Delta Airlines
Delta Airlines · DL991 · LAX → JFK

Hyatt Place · 350 West 39th St, New York NY 10018

Home base in Midtown Manhattan

Hyatt Place Times Square places you at 350 West 39th Street — in the beating heart of Midtown Manhattan. Broadway, Rockefeller Center, Central Park, the Financial District, and Times Square are all within walking distance or a short subway ride.

Students check in independently, managing their own room keys and hotel responsibilities from the moment they step to the front desk. This is by design — your first act as a professional in New York City happens before the summit even begins.

Group stay: April 20–23, 2026 · Group Confirmation: VE International Co 34244905

Airport Security

Navigating TSA Like a Pro

Professional travelers know exactly what to expect before they reach the security lane. Here is everything you need to move through LAX efficiently and without incident.

🎒

Carry-On Rules

One carry-on and one personal item per passenger. Verify your bag dimensions in advance. Overpacking leads to gate check fees and delays for the entire group.

💧

The 3-1-1 Rule

All liquids must be 3.4 oz (100ml) or under, packed in a single clear quart-size bag. One bag per person. This applies to toiletries, drinks, gels, and creams.

💻

Electronics Out

Laptops and tablets must be removed from your bag and placed flat in a separate bin before the X-ray. Keep smaller electronics in your bag unless flagged.

👟

Shoes & Metals

Remove shoes and belt at the lane. Wear easy-on footwear to keep the line moving. All metal — keys, watch, coins — goes in the bin, never on your person.

Required at the Checkpoint

What documents do you need?

TSA accepts a valid U.S. passport, a REAL ID-compliant state ID (look for the gold star), or a U.S. passport card. A standard California driver's license or school ID alone is not sufficient. Have your boarding pass — printed or on your phone — and your ID out before you reach the agent. Minors under 18 are not required to show ID for domestic travel, but a school-issued photo ID is strongly recommended.

TSA Travel Checklist

The National & International Stage

Jacob K. Javits Convention Center

The Javits Center is where Hoover steps onto a stage shared with student firms from across the United States — and international VE firms representing countries from around the world. Industry judges, business professionals, and thousands of competing students will fill this iconic venue. This is what you prepared for.

📋 Apr 22 · Javits

Global Innovation Challenge

Pitch an innovative solution to a real-world business problem to an international panel of judges and industry professionals.

🏪 Apr 23 · Trade Show

Trade Show Exhibition

Present your firm's product or service. Network with visiting companies, international delegates, and business professionals from across the industry.

Explore Javits Center

Industry Programs

Your first loyalty memberships

Two of the most powerful concepts in business are loyalty and recurring value. This trip is your first opportunity to enroll in programs real executives use every single week. Join before departure.

Delta SkyMiles

Earn miles from your very first flight

SkyMiles enrollment is completely free and gives you immediate membership in Delta's loyalty ecosystem. Every mile you fly earns points redeemable for future travel — a direct lesson in how loyalty systems drive customer retention in modern business. This is the same program used by every Fortune 500 executive who flies Delta.

Join SkyMiles Free
World of Hyatt

Points, perks, and hospitality intelligence

World of Hyatt is one of the most respected hotel loyalty programs in hospitality. Enroll before check-in and earn points on your Times Square stay. Understanding how hotels use points to build lifetime customer relationships is a core business concept — and you'll be living it firsthand. The folio noted: no membership credited for this stay. Enroll before you arrive.

Join World of Hyatt

PBIS · Professional Code of Conduct

How we show up — every hour of every day

You are representing Herbert Hoover High School, the Glendale Unified School District, and VE International on a national and international stage. The standard of conduct expected of you is not just school policy — it is the standard of the professional world you are entering.

🤝

Responsible

Own your schedule, your belongings, and your actions at all times. You are responsible for your hotel room key, your room's condition at checkout, and any incidental charges incurred under your name. Professionals arrive early, come prepared, and do not require repeated reminders. That standard applies to you now, in New York City.

🌐

Respectful

Honor every space you occupy — the aircraft, the hotel lobby, the Javits Center floor, and the streets of New York City. Address hotel staff, airline crew, competition judges, and event officials with the same deference you would show any senior professional. Your conduct reflects directly on Hoover and on GUSD.

⚡

Engaged

This is not a vacation. It is a high-stakes competition and professional development experience funded by your school and district. Full participation is required in every session, every challenge, and every interaction. Disengagement, distraction during presentations, or failure to support your teammates is a conduct issue — not just an attitude problem.

🌙 Curfew, Safety & Room Standards

The trip operates under a strict 10:30 PM nightly curfew — Monday April 20 through Wednesday April 22. All students must be in their assigned rooms at curfew and remain there until the following morning's schedule begins. Mr. Calvo, Mr. Spurley, and Ms. Ibrahimi will conduct room checks each evening. There are no exceptions.

Failure to comply with curfew is a serious conduct violation and may result in the student being sent home at the family's expense, with formal disciplinary action filed upon return to Hoover High School.

No room switching. You must sleep in your assigned room every night. Visiting other rooms after curfew is not permitted under any circumstance.

No unsanctioned departures. Students may not leave the hotel after curfew for any reason without explicit chaperone escort and authorization.

Phone availability required. All students must keep their phone on and reachable during overnight hours so chaperones can make contact if necessary.

Professional attire at Javits. Business professional or business casual dress is required at all competition and trade show events. Represent Hoover with pride.

Social media responsibility. Anything you post publicly during this trip reflects on your school, your district, and your professional future. Post with intention and professionalism.

Incidentals are your responsibility. Any damage, room charges, or unauthorized purchases will be billed directly to the guest of record listed on the Hyatt folio for that room.

Financial Breakdown

What's covered, what's yours

Financial clarity is not just a courtesy — it is a professional expectation. Understanding what is provided and what is your personal responsibility is itself a real-world lesson in budgeting and accountability.

Covered by Program

What the Program Provides

Round-trip airfare on Delta Airlines (DL991 and DL752) from LAX to JFK and return. All hotel accommodations at Hyatt Place Times Square for three nights (April 20–23), including all applicable taxes and fees per the official group folio. Charter shuttle transfers between JFK and the hotel. Full summit registration and competition entry fees for all Javits Center events and challenges. Group transportation to and from all official summit activities.

Student Responsibility

Your Personal Costs

All meals and personal food expenses throughout the trip — plan a daily food budget before you leave. Souvenirs, personal shopping, and any entertainment outside of official program events. Hotel incidentals — a deposit hold placed by Hyatt at check-in for the guest of record, released upon clean check-out. Any checked baggage fees beyond what your Delta ticket includes. Personal transportation outside of scheduled group movements.

New York City · April 2026

Welcome home, Tornados.

We are all proud of what you accomplished in New York. Every business leader you admire once stood exactly where you are — at the beginning of something bigger than themselves. You walked those streets with purpose, competed against the best student firms in the country and around the world, and proved exactly what Hoover is made of.

Hoover. Glendale Unified. On the international stage.
